message: wa-upg.14.2: add bounded/redacted match tracing primitive

 {"id":"wa-upg.13.7","title":"Docs: crash-only behavior + crash bundles (what, where, how to triage)","description":"# Task: Document crash-only + crash bundles

## Goal
Make crashes *actionable* by documenting:
- what wa records on crash
- where crash bundles live
- how to triage and reproduce

## Content outline
- Crash-only philosophy:
  - crash is an event with artifacts
  - avoid "mystery" behavior
- Crash bundle contents:
  - what is included/excluded
  - privacy/redaction guarantees
  - size/boundedness guarantees
- Where bundles are written:
  - default directory
  - how to change via config/env
- Operator workflow:
  - check `wa doctor` / `wa status` / `wa triage`
  - view latest crash (`wa crashes` / `wa crash latest` if implemented)
  - attach crash bundle to an incident bundle (if applicable)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A future operator can handle a watcher crash without consulting PLAN.md.
- Docs are explicit about what to share and what not to share.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14","title":"[EPIC] Rule match tracing (explain match for packs/rules/detections)","description":"# [EPIC] Rule match tracing ("explain match")

## Mission
Make rule/pattern matching **inspectable** so humans and robots can answer:
- "Why did this rule fire?"
- "Why didn't it fire?"
- "What evidence was extracted?"
- "What part of the input mattered?" (bounded)

## Why This Matters
Pattern systems inevitably drift. Without explainability:
- false positives/negatives become painful to debug
- users distrust detections
- pack maintenance becomes guesswork

A great "explain match" makes the system feel *trustworthy* and *ergonomic*:
- fast iteration on packs
- faster incident diagnosis
- easier agent automation (robots can cite the trace)

## Scope
This epic is **not** trying to invent a second rules subsystem.
Instead, it hardens and unifies the explainability contract across:
- the **core pattern engine** (trace generation, boundedness, redaction)
- the **robot surfaces** (stable schema, machine-parseable)
- the **human CLI surfaces** (readable traces)
- **diagnostics** (incident bundles include traces when relevant)

### Primary surfaces (already tracked elsewhere)
To avoid duplicate work, these existing beads own the command/tool wiring:
- `wa-nu4.2.1.4` — robot `wa robot rules list/test` + pack linter/drift workflow
- `wa-nu4.3.2.6` — human `wa rules` (list/test/show trace)
- `wa-4vx.10.24` — E2E: rules list/test + pack linter (fixture-first drift)

This epic focuses on *making the trace itself excellent*, and ensuring those surfaces meet the robustness/safety/UX requirements.

## Success Criteria
- A reusable engine-level trace primitive exists and is reused by both robot + human rule-testing surfaces.
- Trace outputs are:
  - schema-validated (stable field names)
  - deterministic (stable ordering)
  - bounded (explicit truncation markers)
  - redaction-safe (no secret side-channels)
- Humans can debug a false positive/negative quickly using `wa rules test`.
- Robots can parse traces and cite them in incident/triage output.

## Non-negotiables
- **Stable schema**: robots must be able to parse traces and depend on field names.
- **Bounded output**: no huge dumps; truncate with explicit indicators.
- **Redaction-aware**: traces and evidence must never leak secrets.
- **Deterministic**: same inputs/config produce same trace output ordering.

## Trace content (design intent)
The trace should answer the "debugging questions" without being a firehose:
- matched `rule_id` / `pack_id`
- which phase matched (quick reject / anchor / extractor / state gate)
- match spans (byte offsets) where applicable
- extracted fields (redacted where needed)
- time/micro-metrics (optional) for diagnosing performance regressions
- near-miss reasons are optional and must be **strictly bounded**

## Testing expectations
- Unit tests + golden fixtures for trace stability and boundedness.
- Schema validation for robot-facing traces.
- E2E coverage through the existing rules E2E script (plus any extensions needed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Explain match outputs are stable, schema-validated, bounded, and redacted.
- The trace is good enough that a human can fix a false positive/negative quickly.
- Robots can programmatically cite a trace in incident/triage output.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14.1","title":"Design: explain-match trace schema, boundedness, and redaction","description":"# Task: Design explain-match trace

## Goal
Define the trace schema and safety constraints.

## Requirements
- Trace schema includes:
  - rule_id
  - match spans (where safe)
  - extracted fields
  - confidence/score (if applicable)
  - evaluation path (which subpatterns matched)
- Boundedness:
  - cap total trace size
  - cap number of spans
- Redaction:
  - never include raw secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema is stable and implementable across robot/human surfaces.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:55:02.154733888Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:26:34.517015323Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T06:26:34.516968735Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-rules","phase-4"]}
-{"id":"wa-upg.14.2","title":"Core: explain-match trace generation in pattern engine (bounded + redacted)","description":"# Task: Core explain-match trace generation

## Goal
Implement the **shared core** for explain-match traces inside the pattern engine.

This task is intentionally scoped to the *engine-level* trace primitive so multiple surfaces can reuse it:
- robot `rules test` output (owned by `wa-nu4.2.1.4`)
- human `wa rules test` output (owned by `wa-nu4.3.2.6`)
- incident bundles / diagnostics (wa-upg.1 + wa-upg.14.4)

## Requirements
- Provide a structured trace object for a match, including:
  - stable identifiers: `pack_id`, `rule_id`, (optional) `extractor_id`
  - evidence pointers (byte spans / named extracts) with truncation indicators
  - gating decisions: why a rule was eligible/ineligible (state gates)
  - boundedness metadata: what was truncated, counts, max bytes
- Redaction:
  - extracted fields and excerpts must pass through redaction
  - ensure "trace" cannot become a side-channel for secrets
- Deterministic ordering:
  - stable ordering of matches and fields
  - stable ordering within trace phases
- Performance:
  - tracing must be cheap when disabled
  - trace generation should be optionally toggled so hot-path detection isn't penalized

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - golden fixtures for trace structure
  - boundedness enforcement (explicit truncation markers)
  - redaction invariants (known secret patterns do not appear in trace output)
- Regression tests:
  - schema validation for the trace object (even if embedded in other command outputs)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A reusable explain-match trace primitive exists in the pattern engine.
- Traces are stable, bounded, redacted, and deterministic.
- Downstream surfaces can embed this trace without duplicating logic.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14.3","title":"Implement wa rules command surfaces (list/test/show trace)","description":"# Task: Implement human rule tracing surfaces

## Goal
Expose rule tracing in human CLI.

## Requirements
- `wa rules list` shows packs/rules.
- `wa rules test <text>` shows matches and traces.
- Output:
  - TTY: readable tables and trace sections
  - JSON: stable schema

## Testing
- CLI contract tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can debug false positives/negatives without digging in code.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14.4","title":"Include rule match traces in incident bundles when relevant","description":"# Task: Include rule traces in incident bundles

## Goal
When an incident relates to a detection/pattern issue, include explain-match traces in the incident bundle.

## Requirements
- If incident kind is "rule mismatch" or includes a detection:
  - include rule trace JSON under `traces/`
  - include minimal evidence excerpts needed for replay

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests for bundle contents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Incident bundles include enough information to debug rule behavior.

 {"id":"wa-upg.14.5","title":"Tests: explain-match trace stability, boundedness, and redaction","description":"# Task: Tests for explain-match traces

## Goal
Prove the explain-match trace is **stable**, **bounded**, and **cannot leak secrets**.

This task intentionally focuses on the *trace primitive* (wa-upg.14.2) and its invariants, not the command wiring.

## Test categories
### 1) Golden stability tests
- Golden JSON fixtures for representative rules:
  - positive match (single rule)
  - multiple matches ordering
  - no match (and optional bounded "near-miss" reason)
- Enforce deterministic ordering of:
  - matches
  - trace phases
  - extracted field keys

### 2) Boundedness tests
- Enforce maximum sizes:
  - max excerpt bytes per evidence item
  - max total trace bytes
  - explicit truncation markers (`truncated: true`, counts, etc.)
- Ensure truncation is **visible** in the trace (not silent).

### 3) Redaction / non-leak tests
- Construct inputs that include known secret-like patterns (API keys, tokens, device codes, etc.).
- Assert the trace never contains the raw secret substring.
- Assert the trace records only safe metadata (counts, hashed summaries, or `[REDACTED]`).

### 4) Schema validation tests
- Validate trace objects against the schema used by robot/human surfaces.
- Ensure schema evolution is intentional (golden fixtures catch accidental drift).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail loudly on:
  - ordering drift
  - schema drift
  - truncation not being explicit
  - any secret substring appearing in serialized trace output
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T22:10:46.557440590Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:53:43.611341525Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:53:43.611199742Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-rules","area-tests","phase-4"]}
